angry.title: It seems you've had a challenging day.
angry.description: Anger is a natural signal, but it's physically taxing. Let's help your nervous system return to a state of calm.
angry.avoid: Excessive caffeine, high-intensity scrolling, and ruminating on the trigger.
angry.suggestions: Try a 10-minute progressive muscle relaxation (PMR) to lower cortisol levels before sleep.

items: Practice 4–7–8 breathing to activate the Vagus nerve
Splash ice-cold water on your face (Dive Reflex)
Slowly drink a glass of cold water
Count backwards from 20 to engage the prefrontal cortex
Progressive muscle relaxation (tense for 5s, release for 10s)
Short burst of activity (20 jumping jacks) to channel adrenaline
Use a stress ball to release motor tension
Gently stretch your neck and jaw (common tension spots)
Shake your limbs to discharge 'fight-or-flight' energy
Brain dump: Write without filtering to externalize the anger
Apply the 'Rule of 5': Will this matter in 5 days? 5 months?
Shift from 'Why me?' to 'What is the most helpful next step?'
Identify the root: Is this anger, or is it underlying hurt/fatigue?
Label the emotion: 'I am feeling anger' (creates psychological distance)
Use 'I' statements (e.g., 'I feel overwhelmed') to avoid defensiveness
Implement a 'Communication Timeout' until your heart rate drops
Lower your volume and slow your speaking rate intentionally
Pause for 3 seconds before responding to any provocation
